questions.question_text: A gym charges a \$40 joining fee, then \$25 for each month of membership, giving $y=25x+40$ where $x$ is the number of months. What does the $25$ represent?

options.text: The cost of each month of membership.

options.why: Reads the rate (gradient) as the starting value; the joining fee is the constant $40$.

options.text: The total cost after one month.
options.why: Confuses the per-month rate with the total $25+40=65$ after one month.
Reads the coefficient as the variable $x$ rather than the cost per month.

questions.solution_text:
The $25$ is the rate, the change for each month.
It is the cost of each month of membership.

questions.question_text: A plumber charges an \$80 call-out fee plus \$60 for each hour worked. Which equation gives the cost $y$ dollars for a job of $x$ hours?

options.why: Swaps rate and starting value — treats the \$80 call-out fee as the per-hour rate.
Subtracts the call-out fee instead of adding the fixed starting cost.
Adds the two amounts into a single rate, losing the fixed call-out fee.

questions.question_text: The cost of a taxi is $y=4x+10$, where $x$ is the distance in kilometres. Find the cost of a $25$ km journey.

options.why: Multiplies $4\times25$ but forgets to add the \$10 flag fall.
Adds the flag fall to the distance first, computing $4(25+10)$.

questions.question_text: The cost of a taxi is $y=4x+10$, where $x$ is the distance in kilometres. A passenger paid \$70. How far did they travel?

options.why: Divides $70\div4$ without removing the \$10 flag fall first.
Divides $80\div4$ — adds the flag fall instead of subtracting it before dividing.
Subtracts $70-10=60$ but forgets to divide by the rate of $4$.

questions.question_text:
[tikz]
\begin{tikzpicture}\begin{axis}[width=7cm,height=7cm,axis lines=left,axis line style={-{Stealth}},xmin=0,xmax=8,ymin=0,ymax=50,xtick={0,1,...,8},ytick={0,10,...,50},grid=both,clip=false,scaled ticks=false,ticklabel style={/pgf/number format/fixed},xlabel={Time (hours)},ylabel={Cost (dollars)}]\addplot[thick,blue!70!black,domain=0:8,samples=2]{5*x+10};\end{axis}\end{tikzpicture}
[/tikz]
The graph shows the cost of hiring a jet ski. Use the graph to find the cost of hiring it for $4$ hours.

options.why: Reads the height of the line at $x=6$ instead of at $x=4$.
Reads the height of the line at $x=2$ instead of at $x=4$.
Reads the $y$-intercept (the booking fee) rather than the value at $x=4$.

questions.solution_text:
At $x=4$ the line is level with $y=30$.
Hiring for $4$ hours costs \$30.

questions.question_text: A candle is $3$ cm tall and burns down by $0.5$ cm each minute, so $y=3-0.5x$ where $x$ is the time in minutes. Substituting $x=15$ gives $y=-4.5$. What does this tell you?

options.text: The model only applies until the candle burns out; a negative height is impossible.
The candle is $4.5$ cm below the table.
options.why: Treats an impossible negative height as a real physical position.

options.text: The candle grows again after it burns out.
options.why: Misreads the negative value as the height increasing rather than the model failing.

options.text: The calculation must contain an arithmetic error.
options.why: Assumes a mistake rather than recognising the model is outside its valid range.

questions.solution_text:
$y=3-0.5(15)=-4.5$
A height of $-4.5$ cm cannot happen, so the model only applies until the candle burns out (at $x=6$).
